core_mechanism: Graph-theoretic characterization of a network's connectivity topology via degree distributions, recurring-subgraph (motif) frequencies, and small-world metrics (high clustering with short path lengths), used to locate hub nodes and functional modules from the connectivity structure alone
brief_reason: The method assembles the system as a graph and computes degree distributions, synaptic multiplicities, motif distributions, and small-world properties to identify central nodes and functional modules, treating the paper's objects purely as a network whose topology is quantified (with linear-systems theory a secondary tool only for propagation).
